summary: Tested backend (19 pytest cases) and frontend (login + sidebar nav + dashboard). Found and FIXED a critical backend bug: MongoDB ObjectId _id was being serialized in POST responses causing 500 on every create endpoint (customers, batches, sales, expenses, tanks, lorries, inventory, payments). After fix, all 19 backend tests pass. Frontend login + navigation + dashboard data binding verified.
action_items: OPTIONAL: Verify Add Customer dialog form fields have data-testid attributes (e.g. customer-name) for easier UI testing; current name input did not match standard 'name' attribute selector. Did not block functionality, just couldn't validate via automated selector. Main agent should self-check via UI.
OPTIONAL: Consider adding a small helper insert_clean() instead of repeated doc.pop('_id', None) lines for cleaner code.
critical_code_review_comments: Original bug RCA: motor's insert_one mutates the passed dict by setting _id (ObjectId). Returning that dict caused FastAPI jsonable_encoder to fail with TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable. Fixed by adding doc.pop('_id', None) after every insert_one(doc).
Invoice number generation uses count_documents+1 which is race-prone under concurrent inserts (could duplicate invoice_no). Suggest atomic counter collection.
datetime.now() (naive local) is used for invoice_no year and dashboard 'today' — inconsistent with now_iso() using UTC. Could give off-by-one-day at TZ boundaries.
create_poultry_sale increments customer.outstanding even when payment_status=='partial' (full total added). Partial payments need a paid_amount field to track balance accurately.
/api/payments allows outstanding to go negative (no clamp).
Customer.delete is soft delete (status='deleted') but GET /customers still returns them — no filter applied.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (added doc.pop('_id', None) after each insert_one to fix ObjectId serialization)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed customers, batches, tanks, lorries, inventory items and 2 RBAC users (farm_staff, driver) on every run. No teardown implemented — may accumulate in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend fully working after _id pop fix. Frontend login + sidebar nav + dashboard verified. Add Customer dialog opens, but its inputs may not use input[name='name']; if testing dialog flows add explicit data-testid on form inputs first. backend_test.py covers auth, customers, poultry (batch+sale+expense), water (tank+adjust+lorry+sale), inventory CRUD+moves, payments, finance pnl, dashboard summary, and RBAC (farm_staff blocked from customers, driver blocked from batches). All 19 PASS.
rca of the issue: All POST create endpoints returned 500. Backend log showed: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable")]. Root cause: motor.AsyncIOMotorCollection.insert_one mutates the passed dict appending the new MongoDB _id (an ObjectId). The endpoints then returned that dict directly, and FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId. Fix: pop '_id' from the doc after each insert_one(doc) call (or refetch with projection). Applied via regex replacement across 11 insert sites in /app/backend/server.py. Re-ran pytest: 19/19 PASS.
